weixin_claw_channel.channel_account.desc: Save the base account settings first, then use account-level scan actions to fetch the QR code and login status. The host only renders a generic staged form and does not understand any Weixin-specific protocol.
weixin_claw_channel.channel_account.fiel: This is the business identifier used only inside the plugin so multiple Weixin Claw accounts can be distinguished.
This value is only used inside the plugin and is not synced back to Weixin.
Controls how often the plugin polls Weixin messages. The default value is already enough, so do not shrink it blindly in the name of responsiveness.
Keep the default first and tune it only after the main flow is stable.
Use stable mode for daily operation and debug mode only for integration work or troubleshooting.
Switch to debug mode only when you are diagnosing problems.
weixin_claw_channel.channel_account.sect: This section stores the persisted account fields. QR codes and temporary login state do not belong in saved config fields.
The login flow is driven by plugin-declared actions and runtime state sections. The host only provides generic rendering.
weixin_claw_channel.channel_account.acti: Generate a fresh QR code and write the current login stage into the runtime state section.
Read the current scan session state to confirm whether the account has been scanned or fully logged in.
weixin_claw_channel.channel_account.runt: This section shows the generic runtime state returned by the latest scan action. QR preview uses preview_artifacts instead of private host fields.
No scan action has been executed yet.
The current login summary will appear here after a scan action runs.
There is no timestamp to show yet.
